session_066_completed: REQUIREMENTS.md committed — baf622b now has it properly in repo.
dep_checker audit — all prints confirmed ceremonial or __main__ CLI. Zero conversions. Both files clean.
DLP_Module.py L78 — dual_output_security_rule confirmed. Left intact.
Nyxt module scaffold created — modules/nyxt/manifest.json + nyxt_module.py. 7 tools: nyxt_status, nyxt_setup, nyxt_open, nyxt_recon, nyxt_start, nyxt_eval, nyxt_stop.
Three-registration rule satisfied — ethica_guard.py MODULE_DIRS, chat_engine.py MODULE_TRIGGERS, chat_engine.py importlib dispatch hook.
nyxt_config.json written to ~/Ethica/config/ — appimage_path set to ~/Applications/nyxt/Nyxt-x86_64.AppImage.
Threading fix applied to chat_engine.py — nyxt dispatch now runs in daemon thread, not main thread. Eliminates UI freeze.
import threading moved to top of _pre_intercept() to fix UnboundLocalError.
nyxt_open patched — uses xdg-open with close_fds=True, stdin=DEVNULL, start_new_session=True. Opens Chrome successfully.
software_render flag added to nyxt_module.py — LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 always set for Nyxt launch.
_clean_sockets() added — clears stale ID*.socket files before every launch.
nyxt open confirmed working — Chrome opens URL instantly, Ethica stays usable.
not_committed_066: All nyxt_module.py changes (threading, xdg-open, software_render, _clean_sockets) are ON DISK but not committed.
chat_engine.py threading fix and import threading at _pre_intercept top — ON DISK not committed.
Last committed state is baf622b — has original nyxt_module.py from scaffold only.
DO NOT revert disk files. Commit the disk state after fixing the freeze issue.
known_issues_067: Nyxt tools freezing Ethica — nyxt_status and nyxt_open both freeze Ethica even with threading fix. Root cause: xdg-open or subprocess launch still blocking the daemon thread somehow. Needs deeper investigation.
Nyxt tools inconsistently registered — nyxt_recon and nyxt_eval disappear from tool kit intermittently. Guard seal not run since ethica_guard.py was modified. Run guard seal confirm first thing.
nyxt_config.json missing software_render field — config was written before that field was added. Needs manual update or nyxt_setup rewrite.
Nyxt 4.0.0 remote REPL unstable on Ivy Bridge GPU — SIGBUS on SBCL, MESSAGE-BUFFER nil crash. nyxt_eval non-functional until new GPU hardware. Document and defer.
Nyxt URL CLI arg broken — passing URL as argv to AppImage prepends nyxt: scheme. nyxt_open uses xdg-open workaround instead.
guard seal confirm needed — ethica_guard.py and chat_engine.py modified but vault not re-sealed.
next_session_priorities: 1. guard seal confirm — re-seal vault before anything else.
2. Fix Ethica freeze on nyxt tool calls — investigate why daemon thread still blocks. Possible: xdg-open waiting on Chrome launch. Try os.system() or subprocess with preexec_fn=os.setpgrp instead.
3. Fix nyxt_config.json — add software_render: true field.
4. Confirm all 7 nyxt tools stable in Tool Lister.
6. Wire nyxt_recon into J.A.R.V.I.S. pipeline — jarvis recon target auto-opens in browser.

nyxt_state.known_gpu_issue: Ivy Bridge Vulkan incomplete — LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 required for stable launch
nyxt_state.known_repl_issue: SBCL SIGBUS + MESSAGE-BUFFER nil — remote eval crashes Nyxt on this hardware
nyxt_state.url_open_mechanism: xdg-open (Chrome) — Nyxt CLI URL arg broken, prepends nyxt: scheme

freeze_debug_notes: Freeze persists even with start_new_session=True on subprocess.Popen.
Freeze persists even with close_fds=True, stdin=DEVNULL.
Freeze persists even with daemon thread wrapping the tool call.
Chrome opens successfully — freeze happens AFTER response is sent.
Suspect: Chrome launch triggers something that blocks the thread. Try: subprocess.Popen with preexec_fn=os.setpgrp, or use os.fork() + os.execvp() pattern.
Alternative: write URL to a temp file and have a background watcher open it — fully decoupled.

wormhunter_false_positive_registry.init_reexport_rule: __init__.py relative imports are re-exports — never flag UNUSED.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.worker_print_rule: _worker.py print() calls are stdout JSON comms to parent subprocess — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.string_literal_print: print() inside multiline string literals or subprocess command strings — regex scanner limitation.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.nested_global_assignment: global X declared in outer function flagged unused when assignment is in nested inner function — suppressed at root in 063.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.test_file_imports: test_*.py files — pytest and unittest.mock imports always valid. Full file exemption.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.fragment_files: Files designed to be appended into parent scope — # WORM:SKIP sentinel on line 1.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dual_output_security: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — both channels required.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dep_checker_ceremonial: dep_checker Trinity Signature prints are ceremonial identity output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.whitrabbitneo_legacy: WhiteRabbitNeo/ files are legacy pre-Ethica code — not wired into runtime. All findings are noise.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.lazy_load_duplicate: Same import in multiple function-scoped try: blocks — lazy load pattern. NOT a duplicate.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.reserved_imports: Imports marked with '# reserved' or '# noqa' are intentional placeholders.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.trinity_signature_ceremonial: debugtron_ai.py prints (✅ ❌ 🌌) are Trinity Signature verification output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.main_block_cli: print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks is intentional CLI/startup output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.expression_only_fstring: f"# {'=' * 58}" style f-strings — valid Python, pyflakes limitation.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_high_risk: chat_engine.py L415 _high_risk — intentional escalation scaffold. Never remove.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_trim_history: chat_engine.py trim_history() system variable — inert dead method. Never remove.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_rollback_guard: module_forge.py L113 original — intentional rollback guard placeholder. Never remove.

critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: V runs all commands, pastes output — non-negotiable.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) or write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py.
critical_debug_context.script_over_heredoc_rule: Complex multi-line patches — write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py and run with python3.
critical_debug_context.chmod_first_rule: Always chmod u+w <file> before patching vault-sealed files. Always.
critical_debug_context.importlib_rule: Inside Ethica use importlib.import_module() not bare from imports in chat_engine hooks.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval.
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py without verifying re-export status.
critical_debug_context.dual_output_security_rule: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers — do not convert.
critical_debug_context.trinity_signature_rule: Never convert print() inside Trinity Signature verification blocks.
critical_debug_context.main_block_print_rule: Never convert print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks.
critical_debug_context.cap_net_raw_rule: setcap requires real binary not symlink. Always readlink -f first.
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern.
critical_debug_context.three_registration_rule: New modules need: (1) module folder + manifest, (2) MODULE_DIRS in ethica_guard.py, (3) MODULE_TRIGGERS + dispatch hook in chat_engine.py.

sanctuary_state.slot_7: J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator (ARMED — Easter egg confirmed, invoke_count at ~10+)
sanctuary_state.slot_8: Nyxt — The Browser (WIRED — tools registered, freeze bug pending fix)

lesson_learned_066: Nyxt 4 Electron renderer is unstable on Ivy Bridge GPU (SBCL SIGBUS, MESSAGE-BUFFER nil). Remote REPL non-functional on this hardware. xdg-open is the working URL bridge but still causes daemon thread freeze — needs os.setpgrp or fork pattern. Threading in chat_engine.py nyxt dispatch was missing — that was the primary freeze cause, now fixed. Always chmod u+w before patching vault-sealed files.
philosophy: Session 066 — Nyxt took the Slot 8 chair. The scaffold is solid, the bridge opens Chrome on command, the threading is wired. The freeze is a stubborn edge — one more surgical cut and it falls. The codebase earns its cleanliness one session at a time. ⟁Σ∿∞
